title: SQL SELECT and Filtering
summary: Querying databases with SELECT, FROM, WHERE, ORDER BY, DISTINCT, LIMIT, and pattern matching.
content_html:
Basic SELECT Syntax

SELECT column1, column2, ...
FROM table_name
WHERE condition
ORDER BY column [ASC|DESC]
LIMIT n;

SELECT All Columns

SELECT * FROM Students;   -- returns all columns

Filtering with WHERE

SELECT Name, Age FROM Students WHERE Age > 20;

Operators: =, !=, <>, <, >, <=, >=
Logical:   AND, OR, NOT

SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Age >= 18 AND Major = 'CS';
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE NOT (Age = 20);

BETWEEN, IN, LIKE

-- BETWEEN (inclusive)
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Age BETWEEN 18 AND 22;

-- IN (match any value in list)
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Major IN ('CS', 'Math', 'Physics');

-- LIKE (pattern matching, % = any sequence, _ = any single char)
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Name LIKE 'A%';   -- starts with A
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Name LIKE '_ob';  -- 3-char ending in 'ob'

NULL Handling

-- Use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL (not = NULL)
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Email IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM Students WHERE Email IS NOT NULL;

DISTINCT

SELECT DISTINCT Major FROM Students;   -- unique majors only

ORDER BY and LIMIT

-- Sort by Age descending, then Name ascending
SELECT * FROM Students ORDER BY Age DESC, Name ASC;

-- Get top 5 oldest students
SELECT * FROM Students ORDER BY Age DESC LIMIT 5;

Calculated Columns and Aliases

SELECT Name, Age * 12 AS AgeInMonths FROM Students;
SELECT s.Name, s.Age FROM Students AS s WHERE s.Age > 20;
